data.title: Three Agencies Rate Digital Realty - One Sees Something The Others Don't
data.description:
Digital Realty Trust Inc (NYSE:DLR) carries three investment-grade credit ratings. Two of them agree. One does not.

S&P Global Ratings assigns BBB+ with a stable outlook. Moody’s Investors Service rates the issuer Baa2, also stable. Fitch Ratings assigns BBB, stable.

The consensus is investment grade. The one-notch divergence is structural.

BBB+ and BBB sit one notch apart. In isolation, the distinction may appear modest. For an issuer carrying approximately $18.4 billion in total debt as of December 31, 2025, one notch can affect index eligibility, counterparty terms, and the cost of refinancing across a multi-year maturity ladder.

The structural question is not whether DLR is investment grade — all three agencies confirm that. The question is what S&P’s framework credits that the other two have not yet recognized.

Source: DLR Q3 2025 Financial Supplement (credit ratings); Fitch Ratings affirmation, March 27, 2025.

What The Numbers Show (As Of Q4 2025)

Net Debt to Adjusted EBITDA: 4.9x

As reported in the Q4 2025 earnings release (February 5, 2026), leverage stood at 4.9x, unchanged from Q3. Management has stated a long-term target of below 5.5x. The current margin to that threshold is 0.6x.

Fixed charge coverage was 4.5x. Debt-plus-preferred-to-total enterprise value was 26.1%. Liquidity stood near $7 billion at quarter-end.

On a leverage basis alone, 4.9x falls within investment-grade territory under agency frameworks. The split does not appear to originate from leverage.

Source: DLR Q4 2025 earnings release (February 5, 2026).

Capital Intensity: approximately 5 gigawatts of buildable IT capacity

This is where data center REITs diverge structurally from traditional net lease. DLR’s development runway spans approximately 5 gigawatts of IT capacity across more than 40 metropolitan areas. As of year-end 2025, the signed-but-not-commenced backlog reached approximately $1.4 billion at 100% share — a company record.

In Q4, $209 million of lease commencements exceeded $175 million of new bookings at DLR’s share. For 2026, approximately $634 million of leases are scheduled to commence ratably through the year.

Capital deployment at this scale can require continuous funding. How each agency weighs capital intensity — as contracted growth or as structural leverage risk — can influence where the rating lands.

Source: DLR Q4 2025 earnings release; Q4 2025 earnings call transcript (February 5, 2026).

Funding Strategy Evolution

DLR’s most significant structural shift in 2025 was the launch of a closed-end private capital fund, which attracted approximately $3.225 billion in LP equity commitments by year-end. Fee income more than doubled during the year, per management commentary.

This evolution can reduce balance sheet capital requirements for hyperscale development. It also shifts EBITDA composition. Fee income, while generated under contractual frameworks, carries different credit characteristics than rental income from stabilized assets.

S&P’s framework may be crediting the diversification of funding sources. Fitch and Moody’s may be weighing the seasoning of this strategy — one year of execution versus a multi-decade rental income track record.

The one-notch gap could reflect that difference in time horizon.

Source: DLR Q4 2025 earnings release; Q4 2025 earnings call transcript (February 5, 2026).

Debt Maturity Profile

In December 2025, DLR repaid early approximately 1.075 billion euros of senior notes originally due 2026. The only remaining 2026 maturity is a CHF 275 million note maturing later this year. Management has described the overall maturity profile as well-laddered through 2037.

The maturity profile does not appear to be a source of rating divergence. All three agencies maintain stable outlooks.

Source: DLR Q4 2025 earnings release (February 5, 2026).

Where The Split May Live

The leverage is consistent across all three rating frameworks. The maturity profile is well-managed. The divergence appears most consistent with how each agency weighs two variables: capital intensity and funding model maturity.

For an issuer deploying capital across a multi-gigawatt development pipeline, the structural question is not whether demand exists — record bookings and AI-driven leasing activity suggest it does. The question is whether the capital required to meet that demand can compress the buffer faster than income restores it.

From a buffer perspective, the one-notch gap may reflect different assumptions about how quickly the capital-to-income cycle converts. If the backlog continues to convert at the current pace, the buffer could stabilize or expand. If conversion decelerates while capital deployment continues, the corridor could narrow — even at 4.9x.

The slope of capital deployment can matter as much as the leverage level itself.

The Structural Read

DLR does not sit near the BBB- boundary. It operates with 0.6x of headroom to its own leverage target, approximately $7 billion in liquidity, and a record backlog scheduled to convert through 2026.

The split rating is not a distress signal. It is a structural disagreement — between agencies that credit a maturing funding model and those still watching the conversion timeline.

That distinction — between consensus and conviction — is where structural diagnosis begins.

The numbers tell a story. Whether it changes anything — that part is yours.

Disclosure: The author holds no position in DLR at the time of writing.

Sources: DLR Q4 2025 Earnings Release, February 5, 2026; DLR Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript, February 5, 2026; DLR Q3 2025 Financial Supplement (Credit Ratings); Fitch Ratings, BBB (Stable), affirmed March 27, 2025; S&P Global Ratings, BBB+ (Stable); Moody’s Investors Service, Baa2 (Stable).

Benzinga Disclaimer: This article is from an unpaid external contributor. It does not represent Benzinga’s reporting and has not been edited for content or accuracy.

data.title: Transocean Shares Pull Back From Highs: What's Driving The Action?
data.description:
Transocean Ltd (NYSE:RIG) shares are sliding Tuesday, as investors appear to lock in gains following the offshore driller's move to a fresh 52-week high late last week.

The stock’s drop comes following news of contract awards that, while significant, have not been enough to offset negative sentiment in the energy sector Tuesday, which is currently the bottom performing sector, down around 1.5%.

  • Transocean stock is taking a hit today. What’s pressuring RIG stock?

Norway Contracts Boost Backlog Visibility

Transocean last week secured contract fixtures for two of its harsh environment semisubmersibles in Norway, expected to enhance its backlog significantly. The Transocean Encourage was awarded a seven-well contract extension, estimated to provide around $152 million in backlog, while the Transocean Enabler has two one-well options exercised, contributing an additional $32 million.

These contracts are set to commence in the first quarter of 2027, ensuring continuity for the rigs’ current programs and extending their operational commitments through December 2027.

The awards expand Transocean's contracted revenue visibility and reinforce its presence in the harsh-environment offshore drilling market.

RIG Trades Below Key Moving Averages

Transocean is currently trading 6.8% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 4.5% below its 100-day SMA, indicating a bearish short-term trend.

Over the past 12 months, shares have increased 72.95%, positioning the stock closer to its 52-week highs than lows, which reflects a strong longer-term performance.

The RSI is at a neutral level, suggesting that the stock is neither overbought nor oversold at this time. Meanwhile, the MACD is below its signal line, indicating bearish pressure on the stock.

The combination of neutral RSI and bearish MACD suggests mixed momentum, reflecting uncertainty in the current trading environment.

  • Key Resistance: $6.50
  • Key Support: $5.50

Transocean Earnings Report Approaches

The countdown is on: Transocean is set to report earnings on Feb. 19.

  • EPS Estimate: 8 cents (Up from Loss of 9 cents)
  • Revenue Estimate: $1.04 billion (Up from $952.00 million)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Hold Rating with an average price target of $6.44. Recent analyst moves include:

  • BTIG: Buy (Raises Target to $10.00) (Feb. 9)
  • Susquehanna: Positive (Raises Target to $5.00) (Jan. 7)

RIG Shares Slide Tuesday

RIG Price Action: Transocean shares were down 6.12% at $6.15 at the time of publication on Tuesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Carvana Q4 Preview: 'Buckle Up' For Earnings, Market Expert Says 'Things To Get Highly Volatile'
data.description:
Here are the earnings estimates, what experts are saying ahead of the report, and the key items to watch.

Carvana Q4 Earnings Estimates

Analysts expect Carvana to report fourth-quarter revenue of $5.23 billion, up from $3.55 billion in last year's fourth quarter, according to data from Benzinga Pro.

The company has beaten analyst revenue estimates in seven straight quarters and in eight of the last 10 quarters overall.

Analysts expect the company to report fourth-quarter earnings per share of $1.12, up from 56 cents per share in last year's fourth quarter.

The company has beaten analyst estimates for earnings per share in eight of the last 10 quarters overall, missing the most recently reported third quarter estimates.

What Experts Are Saying

Freedom Capital Markets Chief Market Strategist Jay Woods highlights expected volatility for Carvana stock.

"Carvana shares have seemed to stall heading into this week's earnings and are at a major inflection point technically," Woods said in a weekly newsletter. "No stock has taken investors on a ride like this one.

Woods highlights the huge movement in Carvana stock with shares peaking at $375 during the COVID-19 pandemic before falling to around $4 in 2022. That drastic downward move was followed by a surge to around $485 in January 2026 before falling to the current level of around $342.

"Expect things to get highly volatile. Over its history, shares have moved +/- 14.2% on average after releasing results. That would equate to roughly a $50 swing given current levels. So buckle up!!"

Woods said a double-top is forming for Carvana stock and a break could take shares back to the $280 to $300 support level.

"Overall the risk far outweighs the reward and is a stock that's exciting to watch but may not be fun for an investor with a low risk threshold."

Here are recent analyst ratings on Carvana stock and their price targets:

  • JPMorgan: Maintained Overweight rating, raised price target from $490 to $510
  • Wells Fargo: Maintained Overweight rating, raised price target from $500 to $525
  • Barclays: Maintained Overweight rating, raised price target from $465 to $530
  • BTIG: Maintained Buy rating, raised price target from $450 to $535

Key Items to Watch in Q4 Results

The earnings report comes after the company recently announced rolling out its same-day vehicle delivery in Oregon's Eugene metro market. The company launched same-day delivery in Arizona and has now expanded to markets in over 20 states for the quicker buying experience for customers.

Carvana shares are down in 2026, with a recent short report from Gotham City Research potentially spooking investors. The short report alleged that Carvana overstated profits during 2023 and 2024. Carvana said the report was "inaccurate and intentionally misleading."

The company is likely to highlight recent and new growth initiatives, along with its continued expansion plans, to encourage investors to think about the future.

Carvana's third-quarter results showed overall revenue up 55% year over year, with 155,941 vehicles sold. The company saw vehicle sales up 44% year-over-year in the third quarter.

The company highlighted industry-leading growth and profitability in the quarter with a goal of selling more than 150,000 vehicles in the fourth quarter.

Future guidance from the company, especially after the latest short report, could make shares volatile moving forward.

Carvana Stock Price Action

Carvana stock trades at $342.96 on Tuesday versus a 52-week trading range of $148.25 to $486.89. Carvana stock is down 14.3% year-to-date in 2026, with shares up 20.5% over the last 52 weeks.

Photo: Around the World Photos on Shutterstock.com

data.title: What's Going On With Algorhythm Stock Tuesday?
data.description:
Algorhythm Holdings (NASDAQ:RIME) stock fell Tuesday as the company sought to steady investor sentiment following volatility tied to its SemiCab platform and broader weakness in the freight trucking industry.

Last Friday, Algorhythm’s AI-driven SemiCab platform rattled the sector, triggering a sharp selloff that erased an estimated $17.4 billion in combined market value.

CEO Emphasizes Collaboration Over Disruption

On Tuesday, Chief Executive Officer Gary Atkinson sought to clarify the company’s positioning, describing SemiCab as a collaborative solution rather than a competitive threat.

“Truckload freight is a $3 trillion global market and the backbone of modern commerce. Yet the industry still operates largely without coordinated intelligence at network scale. When you apply AI across a system of that magnitude, the impact isn’t small, it’s monumental. Our goal is to work alongside the industry to make that coordination practical and repeatable every day,” Atkinson said.

He highlighted structural inefficiencies in the sector, noting that roughly 30% to 35% of freight miles are driven empty, increasing fuel costs, reducing driver productivity, and lowering equipment utilization. Those expenses, he said, are ultimately passed on to consumers.

White Paper Cites Efficiency Gains

According to a company white paper, SemiCab has delivered a 70% reduction in empty miles and throughput gains of 300% to 400% for customers, including Kellanova, Procter & Gamble India, and Asian Paints.

“We are not competing against freight companies, brokers, or shippers — they are our customers and our partners,” Atkinson added. “Our objective is to work collaboratively with them to unlock structural efficiencies through intelligent network coordination.”

Algorhythm said the platform’s traction could support enterprise growth, larger contracts, and geographic expansion into 2026.

RIME Price Action: Algorhythm Holdings shares were down 12.03% at $4.51 at the time of publication on Tuesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: What's Going On With CoreWeave Stock On Tuesday?
data.description:
CoreWeave Inc (NASDAQ:CRWV) shares are trading lower Tuesday. The stock drop comes following disclosure of share sales by CEO and President Michael Intrator.

According to a Securities and Exchange Commission filing, Intrator sold a total of 32,455 shares of Class A common stock worth $7.72 million directly owned by him in multiple transactions on Feb. 11.

The cloud infrastructure provider is seeing its stock pressured as the Nasdaq Composite sheds 0.09% in tandem with broader market volatility.

Strategic Infrastructure Shifts

Nvidia Ties

Sentiment remains largely optimistic following a January upgrade from Deutsche Bank, which moved the stock from Hold to Buy with a $140 price target.

CoreWeave’s Upcoming Earnings

CoreWeave is set to report earnings on Feb. 26. With just nine days to go, the market is keenly awaiting these results to gauge the company’s fiscal health and future prospects.

  • EPS Estimate: Loss of 65 cents (Down from 75 cents YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: $1.53 billion (Up from $1.45 billion YoY)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions

The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $125.70. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Macquarie: Neutral (Maintains Target to $115.00) (Jan. 27)
  • Mizuho: Neutral (Raises Target to $100.00) (Jan. 27)
  • Deutsche Bank: Upgraded to Buy (Raises Target to $140.00) (Jan. 27)

CRWV Price Action: CoreWeave shares were down 5.83% at $90.44 at the time of publication on Tuesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo: T. Schneider / Shutterstock
